title: boronia inornata subsp. inornata
intro: Boronia inornata subsp. inornata, also known as the Western Boronia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the southwestern regions of Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities. The health benefits of Boronia inornata subsp. inornata include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, with its essential oil being extracted for use in aromatherapy and perfumery.
